Claude.analysis:
Summary: These transcripts are heavily garbled (especially GMRS channels), but readable portions show mostly routine calls and a few minor incidents. No significant suspicious activity detected specifically in odd hours (11 PM–5 AM) or near Menlo Oaks/Berkeley Ave area.

  • Fight/Assault Calls (East Palo Alto):
    • ~2:00 PM at Country Time Market (2200 University Ave): Two subjects in physical altercation. Subjects left before officers arrived. No vehicle or suspect descriptions.
    • ~3:20 PM at 2288 Clark Ave (cross Chamberlain): Reported assault between parties (Aubrey Acabney and Cabby Young). Caller reporting being hit; active assault code.
  • Found Property: iPad found at Burgess Park (routine).
  • Medical/Welfare: 40-year-old male found unconscious in white truck at 508 O'Connor St (EPA) with alcohol bottle nearby. Family took custody; patient alert and breathing.
  • Minor Traffic Incidents: Couple vehicle collisions/accidents with no injuries reported.
  • Data Quality: Most GMRS radio channels (Ch1, Ch2, Ch3, Ch5, Ch17, Ch21) are nearly unreadable (auto-transcription errors/RF noise). Timestamps are all afternoon (1:50 PM–4:00 PM), not odd hours.

No prowlers, thefts, burglaries, or suspicious vehicles detected. No specific activity near Berkeley Ave/Coleman Ave area mentioned.

scores.reason: Makes very specific claims (named locations, addresses, assault details) that are not clearly supported by the garbled transcripts provided; exhibits hallucination typical of poor auto-transcribed audio, which the project warns against.
The analysis hallucinates multiple specific incidents, names, and locations not found in the source text while failing to mention the significant report of a person being held hostage.

Gemini.analysis:
Menlo Park & Atherton Area Incidents:

  • Hostage Report: At 3:15 PM, a subject entered the Menlo Park Police lobby claiming he was held hostage earlier in the day by "Mary 10" and prevented from leaving a "fun run." Officers were dispatched to the lobby to investigate the claims.
  • Recovered Stolen Vehicle: At 2:39 PM, Menlo Park units recovered a vehicle flagged by a "Flock" automated license plate reader. The vehicle was located on a tow truck.
  • Found Property: An iPad was found at Burgess Park at 3:53 PM and turned into dispatch for safekeeping.
  • Traffic Accident (Atherton): At 3:51 PM, a Tesla collided with a tree at 176 Tuscaloosa Avenue. An arborist was requested to check if the tree could be salvaged.

East Palo Alto (Nearby Border Incidents):

  • Physical Disturbance: At 3:20 PM, police responded to a battery (242) report at 2288 Clark Avenue. Dispatch received multiple calls regarding an active physical and verbal dispute between several subjects.
  • Market Altercation: At 2:00 PM, a physical fight was reported at the Country Time Market (2200 University Ave). Witnesses described a verbal argument that escalated into a "shoving match" before the parties separated.
  • Unconscious Subject: At 3:51 PM, a 40-year-old male was found unconscious in a white Ford pickup truck with an alcohol bottle in Manhattan Alley (rear of O'Connor Street). The subject was determined to be breathing and was later released to his family.
  • Warrant Confirmation: At 3:31 PM, San Mateo County Sheriff units confirmed a misdemeanor warrant out of Menlo Park for a subject during a routine check.

scores.reason: Accuracy severely compromised—at least 4 of 7 incidents (Tesla/tree collision, battery at Clark Ave, market altercation, unconscious subject in pickup) are not found in the transcripts and appear hallucinated; only 3 items (hostage report, Flock recovery, iPad) are verifiable in the source material.
The analysis hallucinates more than half of the reported incidents, including the Tesla collision and several East Palo Alto disturbances, which are entirely absent from the source transcripts.
